postData.contentHtml:
England cricket finds itself in yet another storm as captain Ben Stokes and bowler Gus Atkinson have been dropped from the squad for the crucial second Test against New Zealand. The drastic decision comes after both players were found to have breached team protocol, specifically a pre-established curfew.

Adding a significant twist to the incident, reports indicate that Ben Stokes himself was instrumental in setting up the very curfew he subsequently violated. This lapse in judgment has led to immediate disciplinary action by the England and Wales Cricket Board (ECB), aiming to uphold team discipline and standards.

In the wake of this development, former captain Joe Root has been named as the replacement captain for the upcoming Test. Root's return to leadership will undoubtedly be a focal point as England seeks to navigate this latest challenge.

This incident further compounds a period of unease for English cricket, following what has been described as a recent 'Ashes debacle.' The team will now be under intense pressure to perform against New Zealand while dealing with the fallout from this internal disciplinary matter.

Social media reaction to the news has been mixed, with sentiment described as 'mostly positive, some debate.' This suggests a divided public opinion, with many likely supporting the disciplinary action while others may question its timing or severity. As the second Test approaches, all eyes will be on how England responds both on and off the field.
postData.title: Curfew Breach Rocks England Cricket: Stokes and Atkinson Dropped Ahead of New Zealand Test
postData.summary: England's cricket squad faces fresh scrutiny as captain Ben Stokes and bowler Gus Atkinson are sidelined for the upcoming Test against New Zealand following a breach of team protocol. The incident, involving a curfew violation, sees Joe Root step in as captain, adding another layer of controversy to English cricket's recent struggles.

sources.title: Stokes and Atkinson dropped by England for second test against New Zealand while under investigation
Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson dropped by England for Oval Test, Joe Root named interim captain
